segments.text: That day Deborah and Barak, son of Abinoam, sang this song:
Israel's leaders took charge, and the people were totally committed. Praise the Lord!
segments.speaker: Deborah and Barak

segments.text: Listen, kings! Pay attention, rulers! I, yes I, will sing to the Lord; I will praise the Lord, the God of Israel, in song.
segments.speaker: Deborah

segments.text: Lord, when you set off from Seir, when you marched from the land of Edom, the earth shook, rain fell from the skies, the clouds poured down water.
segments.speaker: Deborah

segments.text: The mountains melted in the presence of the Lord, the God of Sinai, in the presence of the Lord, the God of Israel.
segments.speaker: Deborah

segments.text: In the days of Shamgar, son of Anath, in the days of Jael, people didn't use the main highways and stayed on winding paths.
segments.speaker: Deborah

segments.text: Village life in Israel was abandoned until I, Deborah, came on the scene as a mother in Israel.
segments.speaker: Deborah

segments.text: When the people chose new gods,  then war arrived at their gates. Not even a shield or spear could be found among forty thousand warriors in Israel.
segments.speaker: Deborah

segments.text: My thoughts are with the Israelite commanders and those people who volunteered. Praise the Lord!
segments.speaker: Deborah

segments.text: You people riding white donkeys, sitting on comfortable blankets, traveling down the road, notice
segments.speaker: Deborah

segments.text: what people are talking about as they gather at the watering holes. They describe the Lord's just acts and those of his warriors in Israel. Then the people of the Lord went to the town gates.
segments.speaker: Deborah

segments.text: ‘Wake up, Deborah, wake up! Wake up, wake up, sing a song! Get up, Barak! Capture your prisoners, son of Abinoam.’
segments.speaker: Deborah

segments.text: The survivors went to attack the nobles, the people of the Lord went to attack the powerful.
segments.speaker: Deborah

segments.text: Some came from Ephraim, a land that used to belong to the Amalekites; the tribe of Benjamin followed you with its men. Commanders came from Makir; from Zebulun came those who carry a military officer's staff.
segments.speaker: Deborah

segments.text: The leaders of Issachar supported Deborah and Barak; they raced into the valley following Barak. But the tribe of Reuben was very undecided.
segments.speaker: Deborah

segments.text: Why did you stay at home in the sheepfolds, listening to shepherds whistling for their flocks? The tribe of Reuben really couldn't decide what to do.
segments.speaker: Deborah

segments.text: Gilead remained on the other side of Jordan. Dan stayed with his ships. Asher sat still on the seacoast, not moving from his ports.
segments.speaker: Deborah

segments.text: The people of Zebulun risked their lives; as did Naphtali on the high battlefields.
segments.speaker: Deborah

segments.text: Kings came and fought, the Canaanite kings fought at Taanach near the waters of Megiddo, but they didn't get any silver plunder.
segments.speaker: Deborah

segments.text: The stars fought from heaven. The stars in their courses fought against Sisera.
segments.speaker: Deborah

segments.text: The Kishon River swept them away—the old river turned into a raging torrent!  I bravely march on!
segments.speaker: Deborah

segments.text: Then the horses' hooves flailed loudly, his stallions stampeded.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: ‘Curse Meroz,’ says the angel of the Lord. ‘Totally curse those who live there, for they refused to come help the Lord, to help the Lord against the powerful enemies.’
segments.speaker: Angel of the LORD

segments.text: Jael, the wife of Heber the Kenite is to be praised the most among women. She deserves praise above all other women who live in tents.
segments.speaker: Deborah

segments.text: He asked for water, and she gave him milk. In a bowl fit for nobles she brought him buttermilk.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: With one hand she picked up the tent peg, and with her right hand she held a workman's hammer. She hit Sisera and smashed his skull; she shattered and pierced his temple.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: At her feet he collapsed, he fell, he lay motionless. At her feet he collapsed, he fell; where he collapsed, there he fell, his life plundered from him.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Sisera's mother looked out from the window. Through the latticed window she cried out, ‘Why is his chariot taking so long to come? Why is the sound of his chariot arriving so delayed?’
segments.speaker: Sisera's mother

segments.text: The wisest of her ladies tells her, and she repeats the same words to herself,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: ‘They're busy dividing up the plunder and assigning a girl or two for each man. There'll be colorful clothes for Sisera as plunder; beautifully embroidered colorful clothes as plunder; double-embroidered clothing reaching to the neck as plunder.’
segments.speaker: Sisera's Mother or Wise Ladies

segments.text: May all your enemies die like this, Lord, but may those who love you shine like the sun in all its brilliance!
segments.speaker: Deborah

segments.text: The land was at peace for forty years.
